name: Flutter / pub.dev
what_it_does: Google's UI toolkit for building natively compiled applications for mobile (iOS/Android), web, and desktop from a single Dart codebase. pub.dev is the Dart/Flutter package registry with a REST API for package metadata, search, and version information.
use_cases: Querying pub.dev REST API for package metadata, versions, and compatibility information
Automated dependency resolution and version analysis via pub.dev API
Building cross-platform mobile/desktop/web apps from a single Dart codebase
Flutter CLI automation for building, testing, and deploying apps in CI
Checking package scoring and null-safety status via pub.dev API
not_for: Teams committed to React Native or other JS-based mobile frameworks
Apps requiring tight integration with platform-specific UI idioms that clash with Flutter's custom renderer
Server-side application development (Dart is capable but ecosystem is small)
Teams uncomfortable learning Dart as a new language
best_when: An agent needs to query Flutter package metadata via pub.dev API, or is orchestrating Flutter CLI builds in a CI pipeline.
avoid_when: Your team is already invested in React Native/Expo, or you need agents to programmatically manage a complex Flutter app build without deep Flutter CLI knowledge.

auth.notes: pub.dev read API (package queries, metadata) requires no authentication. Package publishing requires OAuth2 via Google account. Flutter CLI requires no auth for builds; Firebase/Fastlane integrations have their own auth requirements.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Fully free - Flutter SDK and pub.dev are free with no usage limits
pricing.paid_tiers: None for Flutter/pub.dev. Cloud build services (Codemagic, Bitrise) have their own pricing.
pricing.notes: Flutter is BSD-licensed and pub.dev is free to use. No paid tiers, hosted service fees, or usage caps. Google maintains the infrastructure at no cost to developers.

performance.rate_limits: pub.dev API: no documented rate limits, but abuse prevention applies

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: pub.dev REST API returns clean JSON with standard HTTP status codes. Flutter CLI errors are human-readable but can be verbose. Build errors often require Dart/Flutter domain knowledge to resolve. pub.dev API errors are minimal but sufficient for metadata queries.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: pub.dev API is read-only for most operations, making it inherently idempotent. Flutter builds are deterministic given the same pubspec.lock. No write API operations to worry about for agent use cases.
known_agent_gotchas: pub.dev API is not versioned with a v1/v2 prefix - endpoint structure may change without notice
Flutter SDK version pinning is critical; agents that install 'latest' may encounter breaking changes between stable releases
pubspec.yaml dependency resolution can fail with version conflicts that require human domain knowledge to resolve
Flutter web and desktop targets require additional setup steps not needed for mobile-only builds
The `flutter pub get` command requires internet access and can fail in air-gapped CI environments
Dart null safety migration status varies by package and can cause incompatibility errors at build time

score_rationale: Flutter scores well as a free, open platform with a functional REST API on pub.dev for package metadata queries. However, agent-readiness is limited: the pub.dev API covers only package discovery (not build orchestration), Flutter CLI builds require Dart/Flutter domain expertise to troubleshoot, and there is no hosted build API analogous to Expo's EAS. The platform is less agent-friendly than web-based tooling due to platform SDK requirements (Xcode, Android SDK) that are difficult to manage in ephemeral environments.
